summary:
Tapered posts provide better adaptation and retention when passively cemented, making them a superior choice over screw-in parallel or partially-cemented options for mandibular molars based on reduced stress and improved micromotion compatibility. \ The preference leans towards taper-shaped posts secured through passive cementation as they balance biocompatibility and structural integrity effectively. \$
```
